steps.title: Find the third interior angle
steps.content:
The two given interior angles are $50^\circ$ and $70^\circ$. Since a triangle's interior angles sum to $180^\circ$:
$$
180^\circ-50^\circ-70^\circ=60^\circ
$$

steps.title: Use the straight line
steps.content: The $60^\circ$ interior angle and the exterior angle $x$ form a straight line, so they are supplementary: $$ x=180^\circ-60^\circ=120^\circ $$ The answer is B.
